# Agent Skill Index

Flat catalog of `.agents/skills/`, one line per skill, for fast keyword lookup before opening a `SKILL.md`.
Derived from frontmatter — **not** a source of truth; `AGENTS.md` § Skill Routing stays canonical.
Regenerate with `pnpm agents:index` (staleness is enforced by `pnpm agents:validate`).

94 skills. `[refs:N]` marks skills with N `references/*.md` deep-dive files.

- `admin-list-pattern` — Build searchable, paginated Erify Admin tables; use table-view-pattern for shared mechanics.
- `agent-instruction-maintenance` — Maintain shared agent instructions, adapters, skills, rules, and workflows for routing changes.
- `ai-platform-capability-verification` [refs:1] — Verify deployed Open WebUI or LiteLLM behavior from tagged source and live probes before relying on capability claims.
- `ai-platform-release-management` [refs:1] — Manage Railway upgrades and rollback for Open WebUI or LiteLLM. Use for version, release, and drift work.
- `ai-workspace-control-plane` — Govern Open WebUI, LiteLLM, Better Auth SSO, Railway, and MCP as one control plane for policy or architecture work.
- `api-performance-optimization` [refs:4] — Improve measured erify_api query performance. Use for N+1, over-fetching, aggregation, or pagination work.
- `astro-starlight-best-practices` [refs:1] — Apply Astro and Starlight guardrails in eridu_docs for routes, rendering, auth, search, content, or assets.
- `authentication-authorization-nestjs` [refs:1] — Implement eridu_auth login, sessions, JWT, and cross-app auth; not erify_api permissions.
- `backend-controller-pattern-nestjs` [refs:2] — Build erify_api NestJS controllers with correct routes, guards, Zod responses, UID validation, and payload mapping.
- `backend-large-file-refactor` — Refactor erify_api NestJS files over roughly 600 lines or mixing service, controller, repository, or orchestration concerns.
- `backend-testing-patterns` [refs:2] — Test erify_api services, persistence boundaries, controllers, guards, and orchestration with Jest and real-DB gates.
- `caveman` — Use ultra-compressed technical communication only when the user explicitly asks for caveman mode or minimal tokens.
- `code-quality` — Configure linting, testing, and type safety. Use other skills for repo or design audits.
- `codebase-hardening-program` [refs:1] — Run a multi-PR, behavior-preserving hardening program to audit, slice, verify, and reconcile quality work.
- `data-compatibility-migration` — Preserve frontend compatibility across API cutovers with dual response shapes, fallback accessors, and staged fields.
- `data-validation` [refs:1] — Design backend Zod validation, serialization, external UID mapping, and typed API-to-service transformations.
- `database-patterns` [refs:6] — Implement Prisma soft delete, transactions, optimistic locking, advisory locks, bulk operations, and safe JSONB writes.
- `design-patterns` — Make high-level architecture, layer-boundary, and package-organization decisions, not implementation quality checks.
- `diagnose` — Diagnose hard bugs and regressions through reproduction, minimization, instrumentation, a fix, and a regression test.
- `doc-hygiene` — Keep PRDs, designs, READMEs, skills, and workflows current without process narration.
- `doc-lifecycle` [refs:1] — Reconcile PRDs, designs, and roadmaps when documentation changes status or location.
- `domain-refactor-cutover-strategy` — Plan large domain renames across models, routes, contracts, and UI with phased cutovers and compatibility windows.
- `engineering-best-practices-enforcer` [refs:2] — Audit eridu-services against repo patterns; use code-quality for tooling and SOLID for design.
- `environment-configuration-zod` — Manage backend and frontend environment variables through central Zod schemas with typed defaults and startup validation.
- `eridu-auth-oauth-provider` — Extend eridu_auth as an OAuth2 or OIDC provider for consent, client management, consumer integration, and JWT coexistence.
- `eridu-docs-information-architecture` — Structure eridu_docs by workflow and function when changing sidebars, landing pages, or content placement.
- `eridu-playwright` [refs:2] — Automate eridu-services browser flows with bundled Playwright CLI scripts for navigation, forms, screenshots, or UI debugging.
- `eridu-security-threat-model` [refs:2] — Threat-model eridu-services trust boundaries and abuse paths only on explicit AppSec requests, not general review.
- `eridu-skill-creator` — Maintain repo-local portable Agent Skills and routing; use write-a-skill for generic creation.
- `erify-api-capability-refactoring` — Capability-first placement and evidence-based persistence selection for apps/erify_api NestJS refactors.
- `erify-authorization` [refs:1] — Implement erify_api guards, roles, studio membership, and permissions; not login or JWT flows.
- `excel-creator-mapping` — Map creators from Excel schedules to database shows. Use spreadsheet for workbook-only editing or analysis.
- `fact-extraction-pipeline` — Extend erify_api fact extraction with extractors, SystemFactKey, paired writes, numeric facts, or hydrated scopes.
- `file-upload-presign` — Implement Cloudflare R2 presigned uploads with validation, compression, size limits, routing, and material policies.
- `frontend-api-layer` [refs:1] — Structure React API clients, request declarations, TanStack Query integration, and frontend transport boundaries.
- `frontend-bundle-splitting` [refs:1] — Split large Vite and React bundles into cacheable Rollup vendor chunks when one eager entry dominates load.
- `frontend-code-quality` [refs:1] — Apply React quality standards for lint rules, file structure, naming, form contracts, and components over 200 lines.
- `frontend-error-handling` [refs:1] — Handle React failures with error boundaries, API interceptors, tracking, user-safe messages, and recovery.
- `frontend-i18n` — Implement frontend localization with Paraglide JS and @eridu/i18n for translations, locale routing, and messages.
- `frontend-performance` [refs:1] — Improve measured React performance through code splitting, render control, and Web Vitals work.
- `frontend-state-management` [refs:2] — Design React server, URL, form, and local state boundaries with correct TanStack Query caching and invalidation.
- `frontend-tech-stack` — Apply repo standards for React 19, Vite, and Tailwind v4 when creating or upgrading a frontend workspace.
- `frontend-testing-patterns` [refs:1] — Test React applications with appropriate strategy, infrastructure, component tests, integration tests, and mocks.
- `frontend-ui-components` [refs:1] — Build UI with Shadcn, Radix, and @eridu/ui while matching shipped component, form, and styling patterns.
- `grill-me` — Stress-test a plan through a decision-tree interview only when the user explicitly asks to be grilled.
- `grill-with-docs` — Grill a plan against domain docs and update terminology or ADRs only when the user explicitly requests it.
- `improve-codebase-architecture` — Find architecture opportunities from domain language and ADRs for consolidation, lower coupling, and testability.
- `jsonb-analytics-snapshot` — Build Postgres JSONB read-model snapshots for fast dashboards or historical aggregates outside OLTP tables.
- `knowledge-sync` — Run canonical post-change knowledge synchronization when aligning docs, skills, rules, workflows, and shared memory.
- `litellm-admin-api` [refs:1] — Operate the LiteLLM Management API for models, access, keys, teams, users, budgets, and spend.
- `local-database-cli` — Query local Docker PostgreSQL from an agent terminal for raw SQL, schema inspection, and data verification.
- `monorepo-doc-layering` [refs:1] — Place eridu-services docs in the correct root, app, design, package, or domain layer.
- `observability-logging` [refs:1] — Add structured, privacy-safe logging to erify_api or eridu_auth with correct levels and event selection.
- `openwebui-assistant-adapter` — Design Open WebUI Workspace Models and assistant manifests from repo skills, tool access, and ai/openwebui files.
- `openwebui-extensibility-design` [refs:1] — Choose the Open WebUI mechanism (Function, Tool, Tool Server, or legacy Pipeline) for a new capability, and where its source lives.
- `openwebui-groups-permissions` — Manage Open WebUI groups, permissions, users, and model, knowledge, or tool grants through the API.
- `openwebui-mcp-tool-integration` — Register and verify erify_api MCP, external MCP, or OpenAPI tool servers as Open WebUI connections.
- `openwebui-rest-api` [refs:1] — Operate the Open WebUI REST API for models, users, groups, knowledge, tools, functions, banners, and keys.
- `operations-review-surface` — Build erify_studios operational-day reviews with lean summaries, paginated tabs, URL state, and full-filter exports.
- `orchestration-service-nestjs` [refs:1] — Implement erify_api workflow coordination, transactions, idempotency, locks, and race-safe writes inside capability-owned use cases.
- `package-extraction-strategy` [refs:1] — Decide when shared logic belongs in a monorepo package and prepare multi-consumer boundaries for extraction.
- `plan-workflow-completeness` — Audit multi-PR plans for missing workflow steps and incomplete cycles before phase or workstream sign-off.
- `pr-ready` — Run the canonical pre-merge review and return a READY or NOT READY verdict. Use when explicitly checking whether a PR can merge.
- `pr-ui-screenshot-review` — Capture Playwright PR evidence for rendered frontend diffs, not backend or docs-only changes.
- `prod-data-sync` [refs:1] — Sync production Postgres into local databases when explicit testing needs real cardinalities or edge cases.
- `prototype` — Build a throwaway terminal or UI prototype only when the user explicitly asks to explore runnable options.
- `pwa-best-practices` — Build Vite and React PWAs with correct service workers, offline behavior, installability, and cache coordination.
- `railway-template-config` — Generate startup YAML or JSON config for Railway Docker or template services deployed without Git.
- `repository-health` — Run the canonical periodic repository health review. Use when explicitly auditing cross-repo quality, architecture, and knowledge drift.
- `repository-pattern-nestjs` [refs:1] — Implement private erify_api repositories when complex persistence earns a dedicated provider.
- `schedule-continuity-workflow` — Implement schedule update, validate, and publish continuity with Google Sheets and pending-resolution handling.
- `secure-coding-practices` [refs:1] — Secure erify_api and erify_studios endpoints, uploads, and auth. Use the threat skill for architecture.
- `service-pattern-nestjs` [refs:1] — Implement erify_api capability services with stable typed APIs and evidence-based persistence boundaries.
- `setup-matt-pocock-skills` — Configure issue-tracker, triage-label, and domain-doc context only when explicitly setting up planning skills.
- `shared-api-types` [refs:1] — Define shared keys, Zod schemas, and API types in @eridu/api-types for frontend-backend safety.
- `shift-schedule-pattern` [refs:1] — Extend Studio Shift Schedule CRUD, blocks, calendar alignment, duty coverage, operational days, or task readiness.
- `show-production-lifecycle` [refs:2] — Implement livestream Show relationships, readiness, transitions, cancellation, completion, and assignments.
- `soft-delete-restore` [refs:1] — Restore soft-deleted erify_api records with correct permissions, optimistic conflicts, endpoints, and audit history.
- `solid-principles` [refs:2] — Review React or NestJS design against SOLID boundaries. Use code-quality or the enforcer for tooling and conventions.
- `spreadsheet` — Create, edit, analyze, or format xlsx, csv, and tsv files. Not for database creator mapping.
- `ssr-auth-integration` [refs:1] — Implement SSR auth against eridu_auth with JWT cookies, JWKS, callbacks, and refresh, not IDP internals or API guards.
- `studio-list-pattern` [refs:1] — Build card-based infinite-scroll Studio lists. Use admin-list-pattern for admin tables or table-view-pattern for grids.
- `table-view-pattern` [refs:1] — Build shared server-driven tables, URL state, grids, editing, and saved views, not card-based Studio lists.
- `task-template-builder` — Extend Task Template Builder schema alignment, draft storage, drag-and-drop, validation, or builder UI.
- `tdd` — Build with a red-green-refactor loop only when the user explicitly requests TDD or test-first development.
- `template-system-fact-migration` — Migrate task templates onto SystemFactKey for catalog expansion, missing bindings, or field-type mismatches.
- `to-issues` — Publish tracer-bullet tracker issues only when the user explicitly asks to convert a plan, spec, or PRD.
- `to-prd` — Create and publish a PRD from conversation context only when the user explicitly requests it.
- `triage` — Use the repo triage state machine only when the user explicitly asks to create, review, or prepare issues.
- `ui-mockup-discussion` — Validate undecided frontend UX with rendered @eridu/ui mockups before planning, not after the UX is settled.
- `user-facing-docs` [refs:1] — Create eridu_docs guides, SOPs, FAQs, and onboarding for shipped product features.
- `wiki-knowledge-maintainer` — Maintain ai/openwebui/knowledge through ingest, review, conflict resolution, link repair, routing, and deduplication.
- `write-a-skill` — Create generic Agent Skills with progressive disclosure. Use eridu-skill-creator for repo maintenance.
- `zoom-out` — Give broader architecture or domain context only when the user explicitly asks to zoom out or see the big picture.
